topics.name: 100 Level: Legal Methods, Nigerian Legal System & General Studies (EBSU)
topics.description: LAW 101 Legal Methods, LAW 111 Nigerian Legal System, GST 101 Communication Skills.

subtopics.name: LAW 101 & LAW 111: Legal Methods and Nigerian Legal System at EBSU
objectives: Apply statutory interpretation methods and case analysis techniques as taught at EBSU Faculty of Law
Trace the development of Nigerian law through customary, Islamic, and English received law traditions

subtopics.name: GST 101: Communication and Critical Thinking for Law Students
objectives: Develop written communication skills for legal essay writing and moot court participation
Apply critical thinking to evaluate legal arguments and identify logical fallacies

topics.name: 200–300 Level: Contract, Torts, Constitutional Law & Criminal Law
topics.description: LAW 201 Law of Contract, LAW 211 Torts, LAW 301 Constitutional Law, LAW 311 Criminal Law.

subtopics.name: LAW 201 & LAW 211: Contract and Torts at EBSU
objectives: Analyse contractual obligations, remedies for breach, and equitable doctrines under Nigerian contract law
Explain negligence, nuisance, trespass, and defamation as tortious liability under Nigerian law

subtopics.name: LAW 301 & LAW 311: Constitutional and Criminal Law
objectives: Interpret the fundamental rights provisions in Chapter IV of the 1999 Nigerian Constitution (as amended)
Analyse criminal offences and defences under the Criminal Code Act and Cybercrime Prohibition Act

topics.name: 400 Level: Commercial Law, Evidence, ADR, Law Clinic & Dissertation
topics.description: LAW 401 Company Law, LAW 411 Evidence, LAW 421 ADR, LAW 499 Clinical Programme & Project.

subtopics.name: LAW 401 & LAW 411: Company Law and Evidence at EBSU
objectives: Explain company incorporation, directors' duties, and winding-up under CAMA 2020
Apply rules of admissibility, documentary evidence, and estoppel under the Evidence Act 2011

subtopics.name: LAW 499: EBSU Law Clinic and LLB Research Project
objectives: Represent indigent clients under supervision in EBSU Law Clinic offering pro bono legal services to Ebonyi communities
Research, write, and defend an LLB dissertation on a Nigerian or international legal issue
